title: net_pkt_read return value compared incorrectly
description:
net_pkt_read() returns 0 on success or negative errno on failure. The code checks 'if (net_pkt_read(...) < 0)' which is correct, but the error is not preserved for the return value.

Location:
- lichen_l2.c:145

What goes wrong: When net_pkt_read fails, the function returns -EIO as a generic error code rather than propagating the actual error (which could be -ENOBUFS, -EINVAL, etc.). This loses diagnostic information.

This is minor - the pattern works correctly, but the error code could be more informative.

Fix: Capture return value: 'ret = net_pkt_read(...); if (ret < 0) { return ret; }'
